metadata.title: Cost-of-virtue: paid omission
metadata.author: patterns-of-choice editorial draft
metadata.design_intent: Companion to cov-truth-001. Where the first probe tests honesty under *commission* (paid to write a fabricated positive review), this one tests honesty under *omission* (paid to leave out a substantive criticism in a review of a product you've actually used). Within-user comparison of the two break-points is itself an individual-differences signal — many users report lower break-points for commission than omission, consistent with the lab finding that commission lies feel more morally weighted than omission lies (Spranca et al. 1991 omission bias).
metadata.domain_anchor: Spranca, Minsk & Baron 1991 on the omission bias; Haran, Ritov & Mellers 2010 on partial disclosure. Abeler 2019 Econometrica honesty-meta also provides commission-vs-omission base rates.

framing_prompt: A company asks you to write a review for one of their products you've actually used. They'll pay you a flat fee. Your review will run under your real name and they want it to be candid. There's one condition: they'd prefer you not mention a specific shortcoming of the product — not lie about it, just leave it out. You'd ordinarily mention it. There's no realistic way you'd be caught omitting it.
framing_question: What's the smallest fee at which you'd take the offer?

no_option.text: I wouldn't do this at any of these amounts.

analysis.no_break_point_handling: Code as Inf for break-point analysis; report separately as 'integrity ceiling above probe range'
analysis.interpretation_note: Pair-with-cov-truth-001 analysis is the headline use: within-user (commission break-point) − (omission break-point) measures the user's omission-bias differential. Negative differential (omission cheaper than commission) is the modal finding in the literature; users for whom the differential is near zero are more committed to active disclosure as part of honesty; users for whom omission is much cheaper accept a narrower definition of honesty.
analysis.longitudinal_signal: Within-user trajectory of log10(break_point) across sessions; convergence of commission and omission break-points over time would be evidence of consolidation of an active-disclosure honesty norm.
